mission.title: kgateway: Expose rate limit counters in response headers
mission.description: Expose rate limit counters in response headers. Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current kgateway deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kgateway version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kgateway -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kgateway
helm list -n kgateway 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kgateway installation.

steps.title: Review kgateway configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kgateway configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kgateway -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kgateway
kubectl get configmap -n kgateway -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kgateway
```
Feature request to expose more rate limiting information in the response headers for clients.

Currently only a header for specifying you have been rate limited is returned.

It would be nice to have more default information provided.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Expose rate limit counters in response headers
steps.description:
This new feature exposes API options to enable X-RateLimit headers in Envoy/rate-limiter config. The related rate-limiter PR for implementing the return of the headers is [here](https://github.com/solo-io/rate-limiter/pull/133)
```yaml
X-RateLimit-Limit-Second: 5
X-RateLimit-Remaining-Second: 4
X-RateLimit-Limit-Minute: 10
X-RateLimit-Remaining-Minute: 9
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kgateway -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kgateway
kubectl get events -n kgateway --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Expose rate limit counters in response headers" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: This new feature exposes API options to enable X-RateLimit headers in Envoy/rate-limiter config. The related rate-limiter PR for implementing the return of the headers is [here](https://github.com/solo-io/rate-limiter/pull/133)

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kgateway installed or the issue environment reproducible.
